story.tasks: - Task 1: Create UsersController with admin endpoints (GET /users, GET /users/:id) - Task 2: Create DTOs (CreateUserDto, UpdateUserDto, QueryUserDto) - Task 3: Implement POST /users endpoint (user creation with password hashing) - Task 4: Implement PATCH /users/:id endpoint (partial update) - Task 5: Implement DELETE /users/:id endpoint (soft-delete) - Task 6: Extend UsersService with admin operations (findAll, create, update, softDelete) - Task 7: Create PERMISSIONS constant and ActionEnum - Task 8: Create @Permission decorator - Task 9: Create PermissionsGuard - Task 10: Create AuthorizationService stub (returns true) - Task 11: Register UsersController in UsersModule - Task 12: Write unit tests for UsersController - Task 13: Update UsersService unit tests - Task 14: Write E2E tests for admin user CRUD

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-3.2.1: GET /users endpoint returns paginated user list - Admin with USERS.VIEW permission can access - Pagination: page (default 1), limit (default 20, max 100) - Filtering: status (active/inactive), role, search (firstName, lastName, phoneNumber) - Sorting: sortBy (default createdAt), sortOrder (default desc) - domainID isolation enforced - Response includes meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages } - Soft-deleted users excluded - Returns 403 if no USERS.VIEW permission
AC-3.2.2: GET /users/:id returns single user - Admin with USERS.VIEW permission can access - domainID check enforced (403 if different domain) - Returns 404 if not found or soft-deleted - passwordHash excluded from response
AC-3.2.3: POST /users creates new user - Admin with USERS.CREATE permission can access - Validates: phoneNumber (unique per domain), firstName, lastName, role - password required if role=admin, optional for staff - Password hashed with bcrypt (10 rounds) - User created with phoneVerified=false - Returns 201 Created with UserResDto - Returns 409 if phoneNumber exists, 400 if validation fails
AC-3.2.4: PATCH /users/:id updates user - Admin with USERS.UPDATE permission can access - Partial update: firstName, lastName, phoneNumber, isActive - domainID check enforced - phoneNumber uniqueness validated - Returns updated UserResDto - Returns 404 if not found, 403 if wrong domain
AC-3.2.5: DELETE /users/:id soft-deletes user - Admin with USERS.DELETE permission can access - Soft delete: set deletedAt timestamp - domainID check enforced - Cascade: UserRole, UserPermission soft-deleted - Returns 200 OK with success message - Returns 404 if not found, 403 if wrong domain

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Controller-Service pattern, module structure, permission system design (hybrid enum + DB), authorization flow, security constraints, multi-tenancy enforcement

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Complete API specs for GET /users, GET /users/:id, POST /users, PATCH /users/:id, DELETE /users/:id with request/response formats

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: CreateUserDto, UpdateUserDto, QueryUserDto, UserResDto specifications with class-validator decorators

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Permission-based access control, multi-tenancy isolation, password hashing (bcrypt 10 rounds), input validation, soft-delete pattern

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Unit tests (85% coverage), integration tests, E2E tests covering all CRUD operations, permission checks, multi-tenancy isolation

doc.title: Architecture Decisions
doc.snippet: Module-based permission constants (PERMISSIONS.MODULE.ACTION), database-backed permission checks, decorator-based route protection

doc.title: Story 3.1 (Previous)
doc.snippet: UsersService foundation ready (findOne, updateProfile), UserResDto pattern established, multi-tenancy pattern, JWT authentication working, validation infrastructure ready

artifact.reason: Existing service with findOne, updateProfile methods. Story 3.2 extends with findAll, create, update, softDelete. Demonstrates multi-tenancy (domainID filtering), soft-delete pattern (deletedAt: null), Prisma usage
Response DTO for all user endpoints. Uses @Expose() to whitelist safe fields, excludes passwordHash/domainID/deletedAt. Reuse for Story 3.2 responses

Reference implementation for controller patterns: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), @CurrentUser decorator, plainToInstance transformation, error handling. Story 3.2 follows same pattern with added PermissionsGuard
Current module config. Story 3.2 adds UsersController to controllers array and imports PermissionsModule for authorization
Validation pattern reference: @IsOptional, @IsString, @MinLength, @MaxLength, @IsPhoneNumber('TR'). Story 3.2 creates similar DTOs
JWT authentication guard supporting @Public() bypass. Story 3.2 uses with PermissionsGuard: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard)
Extracts JwtPayload from request.user. Story 3.2 uses to get domainID for multi-tenancy filtering in all admin endpoints
Pattern reference for creating @Permission decorator. Shows SetMetadata usage and metadata key constant pattern
JWT payload structure (sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles). Used by @CurrentUser decorator and PermissionsGuard
Password hashing pattern: bcrypt.hash(password, 10) with 10 rounds. Story 3.2 replicates in CreateUserDto handler
domainID retrieval from config (DEFAULT_DOMAIN_ID pattern). Story 3.2 may reference for default domain handling
Complete validation reference for CreateUserDto: @IsPhoneNumber('TR'), @IsEnum(['admin', 'staff', 'manager']), @Matches for password, @IsEmail
User entity definition: role (admin/staff/manager), isActive, deletedAt (soft-delete), domainID (multi-tenancy), passwordHash (optional), indexes. Story 3.2 queries based on this schema

artifact.symbol: Permission, Role, UserRole, RolePermission, UserPermission models
artifact.reason: Authorization schema for permission system. Story 3.2 creates foundation code (constants, guards) that will query these tables in Story 3.5
PrismaClient wrapper injected into UsersService for all database operations
Global ValidationPipe configuration (whitelist: true, forbidNonWhitelisted: true, transform: true). Story 3.2 DTOs validated automatically
E2E testing pattern reference: app bootstrap, PrismaService injection, test user creation, JWT token generation, cleanup. Story 3.2 creates similar tests for admin endpoints
Unit test pattern: mocking PrismaService, TestingModule setup. Story 3.2 adds tests for new methods (findAll, create, update, softDelete)
Permission structure: module + action (USERS.CREATE, USERS.VIEW, USERS.UPDATE, USERS.DELETE). Story 3.2 PERMISSIONS constant follows this pattern

nodejs.package: NestJS core decorators, guards, pipes, interceptors
Prisma ORM client for database access

story-context.constraints:
1. **Multi-Tenancy Mandatory:** ALL queries MUST filter by domainID from @CurrentUser() decorator. Never allow cross-domain data access.
2. **Soft-Delete Only:** DELETE operations set deletedAt timestamp, never hard delete. All queries filter deletedAt: null.
3. **Permission-Based Authorization:** All admin endpoints MUST have @Permission decorator. PermissionsGuard executes after JwtAuthGuard.
4. **Password Security:** Hash with bcrypt.hash(password, 10). Never return passwordHash in responses (UserResDto excludes it).
5. **Validation Required:** All DTOs use class-validator decorators. ValidationPipe is global, validation automatic.
6. **Response Format:** Use plainToInstance(UserResDto, user, { excludeExtraneousValues: true }). ResponseTransformInterceptor wraps in { success, data } format.
7. **Controller-Service Pattern:** Controllers are thin (parameter extraction, response wrapping). Business logic in services.
8. **Phone Number Validation:** Use @IsPhoneNumber('TR') for Turkish phone numbers. Enforce uniqueness per domain.
9. **Pagination Defaults:** page=1, limit=20, max=100. Response includes meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages }.
10. **Role Enum:** ['admin', 'staff', 'manager']. Validate with @IsEnum in DTOs.
11. **Error Handling:** Throw domain exceptions (NotFoundException, ConflictException, ForbiddenException). Global exception filter handles mapping to HTTP status codes.
12. **Testing Coverage:** 85% unit test coverage target. E2E tests for all CRUD operations, permission checks, multi-tenancy isolation.
13. **Stub Implementation:** AuthorizationService.hasPermission() returns TRUE for all checks in Story 3.2. Full implementation deferred to Story 3.5.
14. **Module Organization:** UsersModule imports PrismaModule (global), AuthModule (for guards), PermissionsModule (for authorization). Export UsersService for cross-module usage.

interface.name: GET /users
interface.returns: { success: true, data: UserResDto[], meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages } }

interface.name: POST /users
interface.signature: POST /users { phoneNumber, password?, firstName, lastName, email?, role }

interface.name: UsersService.findAll
interface.signature: async findAll(queryDto: QueryUserDto, domainID: string): Promise<{ data: User[], total: number }>
interface.description: Paginated user list with filtering (status, role, search) and sorting. Filters by domainID and deletedAt: null

interface.name: UsersService.create
interface.signature: async create(createUserDto: CreateUserDto, domainID: string): Promise<User>
interface.description: Creates user with password hashing (bcrypt 10 rounds), phoneNumber uniqueness check per domain

interface.name: UsersService.update
interface.signature: async update(id: string, updateUserDto: UpdateUserDto, domainID: string): Promise<User>
interface.description: Partial update with domainID check, phoneNumber uniqueness validation

interface.name: UsersService.softDelete
interface.signature: async softDelete(id: string, domainID: string): Promise<void>
interface.description: Sets deletedAt timestamp, enforces domainID check

interface.name: AuthorizationService.hasPermission
interface.signature: async hasPermission(userID: string, domainID: string, permission: string): Promise<boolean>
interface.description: STUB: Returns TRUE for all checks. Full implementation in Story 3.5

interface.name: @Permission decorator
interface.description: Metadata decorator for route-level permission requirements. Used with PermissionsGuard

interface.name: PermissionsGuard
interface.description: Extracts @Permission metadata, calls AuthorizationService.hasPermission(), throws ForbiddenException if denied

interface.name: PERMISSIONS constant
interface.signature: export const PERMISSIONS = { USERS: { CREATE: 'USERS.CREATE', VIEW: 'USERS.VIEW', UPDATE: 'USERS.UPDATE', DELETE: 'USERS.DELETE' } } as const
interface.description: Type-safe permission constants matching database permission format (module.action)

interface.name: ActionEnum
interface.signature: enum ActionEnum { CREATE = 'CREATE', VIEW = 'VIEW', UPDATE = 'UPDATE', DELETE = 'DELETE' }

tests.standards:
**Unit Tests (85% coverage):** - Mock dependencies with jest.fn() and TestingModule - Test services: PrismaService mocked, business logic validated - Test controllers: Service mocked, HTTP layer validated - Test guards: Reflector and AuthorizationService mocked
**Integration Tests:** - Full request/response cycle with test database - Transaction rollback in afterEach for cleanup - Validate pagination, filtering, sorting - Test multi-tenancy isolation (cross-domain access denied) - Verify soft-delete behavior
**E2E Tests:** - Full app bootstrap with AppModule - Real database with cleanup - JWT token generation via TokenService - Test complete user journeys (create → list → update → delete) - Permission checks (403 when no permission)
**Reference:** test/users-profile.e2e-spec.ts, src/modules/users/services/users.service.spec.ts
tests.locations: - src/modules/users/controllers/users.controller.spec.ts (unit tests for controller) - src/modules/users/services/users.service.spec.ts (extend with new method tests) - src/common/guards/permissions.guard.spec.ts (unit tests for guard) - src/modules/permissions/services/authorization.service.spec.ts (unit tests for stub) - test/users-admin.e2e-spec.ts (E2E tests for all admin CRUD endpoints)
tests.ideas:
**AC-3.2.1 (GET /users list):** - Test pagination: page=1, limit=20 returns correct subset - Test filtering: status=active excludes inactive users - Test filtering: role=staff returns only staff users - Test search: search=john matches firstName/lastName/phoneNumber - Test sorting: sortBy=createdAt&sortOrder=desc returns newest first - Test multi-tenancy: Domain A admin cannot see Domain B users (403) - Test soft-delete: Deleted users excluded from results - Test permission: No USERS.VIEW permission → 403 Forbidden
**AC-3.2.2 (GET /users/:id):** - Test success: Returns UserResDto with passwordHash excluded - Test domainID check: Cross-domain access → 403 Forbidden - Test not found: Non-existent ID → 404 Not Found - Test soft-deleted: Deleted user → 404 Not Found
**AC-3.2.3 (POST /users create):** - Test success: User created with password hashed (bcrypt) - Test validation: Missing firstName → 400 Bad Request - Test uniqueness: Duplicate phoneNumber in same domain → 409 Conflict - Test role validation: Invalid role → 400 Bad Request - Test password hashing: Verify bcrypt.compare() works on stored hash - Test permission: No USERS.CREATE permission → 403 Forbidden
**AC-3.2.4 (PATCH /users/:id update):** - Test partial update: Only firstName provided → only firstName updated - Test phoneNumber uniqueness: Duplicate phoneNumber → 409 Conflict - Test domainID check: Cross-domain update → 403 Forbidden - Test not found: Non-existent ID → 404 Not Found
**AC-3.2.5 (DELETE /users/:id soft-delete):** - Test soft-delete: deletedAt timestamp set, not hard deleted - Test cascade: UserRole, UserPermission records soft-deleted (if exist) - Test domainID check: Cross-domain delete → 403 Forbidden - Test not found: Non-existent ID → 404 Not Found - Test list after delete: Deleted user not in GET /users results
